problem_statement: Nucleus envelope was not reading correctly in Roblox Studio.

findings.finding: A duplicate raw package named EXPORT_LOD0_NucleusEnvelope was visible directly under Workspace, outside MeshLibrary and Nucleus_Model.
findings.impact: The viewport showed a large selected raw shell over the assembled model, making the assembly look scattered or misaligned.
findings.fix: Assembler now moves top-level EXPORT_* orphans into Workspace.MeshLibrary/Nucleus_Phase07R_OrphanWorkspaceImports and hides them without deleting them.

findings.finding: Roblox assembly flattened all envelope subcomponents to the same material, color, and transparency.
findings.impact: Outer membrane, inner membrane, lumen, and surface wrinkles lost biological separation.
findings.fix: Assembler now applies name-based envelope material pass: outer purple membrane, darker inner membrane, neon magenta lumen, darker wrinkle/ridge accents.

remaining_review_process: Viewport inspect Nucleus_Model only, with MeshLibrary collapsed/hidden.
Envelope pass: check double membrane readability, cutaway edge thickness, lumen visibility, and pore seating.
Biology pass: verify lamina is inside inner membrane, pores are selective gates not open holes, and ER connects to outer membrane only.
Roblox pass: confirm transparent lumen does not sort badly from front/back camera angles and collision proxy remains invisible.
If envelope still fails visually, regenerate or re-export envelope as three separate Roblox packages: OuterMembrane, InnerMembrane, PerinuclearLumen.
